title: Parosela quinqueflora

Parosela, also known as Senna quinquefolia, is a flowering plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and constipation. The bioactive constituents of Parosela include sennosides, anthraquinones, and other glycosides, which are responsible for its laxative properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Parosela include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat digestive problems and promote bowel movement.
